titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 2

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=95 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:27:58.705396+00 | translation moa=94 status=live modified=2016-06-07 09:02:31.620063+00

div.p:
W'. Dei gratia Rex Scott'. Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus Iusticiis . Vicecomitibus . Ministris . ⁊
Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue . Clericis ⁊ laicis ⁊ Salutem. Sciant presentes ⁊ futuri me con
cessisse . ⁊ hac Carta mea confirmasse Deo . ⁊ Ecclesie Sancti Andree Apostoli ⁊ Canonicis ibidem seruienti
bus deo ⁊ in perpetuum seruituris terram ad hebrecces . quam Walterus filius philippi eis in perpetuam elemosi
am dedit concessit . ⁊ Confirmauit . Cum communi pastura . Tenend' perpetualiter Ita libere ⁊ quiete . ab
omni seruicio ⁊ consuetudino ⁊ exactione . sicut fratres de hospitali ⁊ de templo vbique tenent . ⁊ sicud
Carta predicti Walteri filii philippi testatur ⁊ confirmat. Test'. Ric' Episcopo de Dunkeld' . Waltero de Bid'.
Cancellario . Comite Dunec'. Ric' de Moreuill' constabulario. Waltero de Winles'. Galfrido de Maleuill'. apud Kingor.

William by the grace of God King of Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, officials, and all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay [-and] Greeting: Let present and future know that I have granted, and by this my charter made firm to God, and to the Church of Saint Andrew the Apostle, and to the Canons there serving God, and who shall serve there in perpetuity, the land at 'Hebrecces' that Walter son of Philip has given, granted and made firm in perpetuity to them, with common pasture, to be held perpetually as freely and quietly from every service and custom and exaction as the brethren of the Hospital of the Temple ever hold, and just as the charter of the aforesaid Walter son of Philip bears witness and makes firm. As witnesses: Richard Bishop of Dunkeld, Walter de Bidun the Chancellor, Earl Duncan, Richard de Morville the Constable, Walter of Windsor, Geoffrey de Malville. At Kinghorn.
